rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rapid-American Corporation¿s motion for terminating sanctions Against Plaintiffs Linda Brecht And Christopher Brecht: hearing required. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY COMBS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Linde, Inc. For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Linde, Inc., fka The BOC Group, Inc. and /or Airco, Inc.¿s motion for a protective order is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK JOHNSON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER, PERMANENT PROTECTIVE ORDER
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Siemens Energy & Automation, Inc.¿s motion for protective order is granted in part and denied in part. The deposition may be taken at a location within 30 miles of Siemens¿ designated COR/PMK and all parties may appear by telephone. Siemens must produce all non-privileged responsive documents at least five days before the deposition and its PMK may be deposed as to all categories listed in the notice. As to 1960 through 1969, there should be no burden to Siemens since it claims not to have been in existence at that time. As to any later period, Siemens has not demonstrated that compliance will involve any undue burden. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WALLACE CAWELTI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant American Honda Motor Co. Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.¿s motion to compel discovery responses from plaintiffs is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM KRAUSMANN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANT(BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Renewed Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs To Produce Pathology For Analysis Of Asbestos Fiber Content
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 5. Defendant W.W. Grainger, Inc.¿s motion for an order compelling plaintiffs to produce pathology For Analysis Of Asbestos Fiber Content is granted. Plaintiffs have not shown that they would be prejudiced by the method that Dr. Roggli proposes to divide the pathology material. Nor is Grainger required to seek approval of any other defendant to proceed as they seek to do. All remaining defendants have been served with the motion and none has opposed it. GO 41 does not bar this motion. = (220/HEK)
